ab: karuwa ku kivelas za paisu kaninu?
en: A: Can I borrow money from you?

ab: kemazu ziya maw! maranger u kivelas za manueman paisu?
ch: B:為什麼不可以啊!你需要借多少錢?

ab: malalup ku mada za paisu, kararuwa ku kivelas za paisu kaninu?
ch: A:我忘記帶錢了，我可以跟你借錢嗎?
en: A: I forgot to bring money, can I borrow money from you?

ab: yi, ’azi ku mada za paisu wa!
en: B: I don’t have any money either!

ab: malalup ku mada za paisu, kararuwa ku kivelas za paisu kaninu?
ch: A:我忘記帶錢，我可以跟你借錢嗎?
en: A: I forgot to bring money, can I borrow money from you?

ab: inau, nanu kinivelas na paisu ’azi u ziya palauli’ kaninku.
ch: B:不要，你借的錢都還沒還我。
en: B: No, you haven’t paid me back the money you borrowed.

ab: kararuwa ku kivelas kananu kavang awa?
en: A: Can I borrow your clothes?

ab: ai, alaku inu.
en: B: Well, you take it yourself.

ab: kararuwa ku kivelas kananu valray awa?
en: A: Can I borrow your book?

ab: ku pavelasanay lra kani Punhi.
en: B: I lent it to Punhi.

ab: alra pavelasay ku kana nu cidinsiya mu?
ch: A:請借給我你的腳踏車好嗎?
en: A: Can I borrow your bicycle, please?

ab: ai , maw mu nu ’emanan paveliyasu kaninku.
ch: B: 好，但是明天要還給我。
en: B: Okay, but you have to give it back to me tomorrow.

ab: ’ema’udalr lra zi, ’azi ku mada za suku, pavelasay ku za suku mu?
ch:
A:
正在下雨了，我沒有帶傘，借我傘好嗎?
en: A: It’s raining. I don’t have an umbrella. Can I borrow one?

ab: maw maw saya a ku suku zi, salrangana ta misuku!
ch: B: 我只有一把傘，我們一起撐吧!
en: B: I only have an umbrella, let’s stick together!

ab: ini na valray mu maw nanu ipavelas kaninku a daman zi ku palauli’aw kaninu.
ch: A:這是你上次借我的書，我還給你。
en: A: This is the book you borrowed from me last time, I’ll give it back to you.

ab: nu marangher u ziya mena’u za eman valrayan kararuwa u marengay kaninku.
ch: B:還想看什麼書都可以跟我說。
en: B:You can tell me what else you want to read.

ab: maw nu aidan pavelaliyas ku za paisu kaninu?
en: A: When do I have to pay you back?

ab: muziya za eman, nu ulra lra nu paisu mu rengay lra.
ch: B:沒關係，你有錢的時候再說。
en: B: It’s okay, you can talk about it when you have money.

ab: nu aidan pavelaliyas u za paisu kaninku?
en: B: When are you going to pay me back?

ab: ku paveliyasaw nu ’emanan kaninu.
en: A: I’ll return it to you tomorrow.

ab: nu aidan palaauli’ u kananku valray?
en: B: When are you going to return my book?

ab: ku palauli’aw lra adaman kaninu kemaku?
ch: B:我昨天不是已經還你了嗎?
en: A: Didn’t I return it to you yesterday?

ab: ini na tratruli mu tu ipavelas ni Risem kaninu?
en: A: Did Risem lend you this pen?

ab: ai, ninku kinivelas kanintaw harem kana wari.
en: B: Yes, I borrowed it from him today.

ab: ini na tratruli mu nu kinivelas kani Risem?
en: A: Did you borrow this pen from Risem?

ab: melri, ini mu tu trinima’an ni nani.
en: B: No, this is what mom bought.

ab: iniyan lra za ’ateri uwa kivelas kani Imay za saeman za ’ateri.
en: A: We’re out of salt, let’s go borrow some from Imay.

ab: ai, kay ku kivelasa.
en: B: Okay, I’ll borrow it.

ab: uwa rengayu i Panay za palauli’an za ku paisu.
en: B: Go tell Panay to give me back my money.

ab: manueman paisu atu kiyam kaninu?
en: A: How much does she owe you?
